channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?' New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, NewsNation

item.title: 5 shot near Howard University homecoming week; 2 people in custody
item.description: Five people were hospitalized after a shooting near Howard University Friday, according to the DC Fire and EMS Department.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Dillon, Fox 5

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: Hegseth’s order comes as the Trump administration has been striking alleged drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa to become a hurricane Saturday and rapidly intensify: NHC
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Tropical Storm Melissa is gaining strength as it remains nearly stationary near Jamaica, the National Hurricane Center said Friday. Melissa is located about 345 miles southeast of Kingston, Jamaica. Maximum sustained winds are 65 mph.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump-Kim meeting speculation flares ahead of US president's visit to South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The last time U.S. President Donald Trump visited South Korea in 2019, he made a surprise trip to the border with North Korea for an impromptu meeting with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to revive faltering nuclear talks.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio chemists warn of rising carfentanil cases as drug mixtures grow more dangerous
item.description: Authorities across the U.S. are warning of carfentanil, a dangerous synthetic opioid 10,000 times more potent than morphine and 100 times stronger than fentanyl.
item.dc:creator: Savannah Young, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey runaway found in Louisiana. Police make human trafficking arrest
item.description: Detectives from the Vice Unit have also launched an investigation into the human trafficking aspects of the case, resulting in further arrests.
item.dc:creator: Erykah Agers, NewsNation

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa lumbers through the Caribbean, killing an elderly man in Haiti
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa lumbered through the Caribbean Sea on Thursday, bringing a risk of dangerous landslides and life-threatening flooding to Jamaica and southern Hispaniola — an island shared by the Dominican Republic and Haiti.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Louisiana jury awards more than $40 million to family of man who died in privately-run jail
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A federal jury found a private company running a Louisiana jail liable for the 2015 death of a man who died of head injuries he received while in custody, and awarded the family more than $40 million in damages. Attorneys representing Erie Moore Sr.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NBA head coach and player charged in sprawling sports betting and Mafia-backed poker schemes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers and a player for the Miami Heat were arrested Thursday along with more than 30 other people in a takedown of two sprawling gambling operations that authorities said leaked inside information about NBA athletes and rigged poker games backed
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, TIM REYNOLDS and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alabama executes man with nitrogen gas for 1993 murder over $200 drug debt
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted of helping to burn a man alive in 1993 over a $200 drug debt was executed by nitrogen gas on Thursday. Anthony Boyd, 54, was pronounced dead at 6:33 p.m. at William C. Holman Correctional Facility, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia Democrats will try to reshape US House districts in counter to Trump's redistricting push
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Virginia Democrats are taking steps to redraw their state’s U.S. House districts, hoping to boost their party’s chances in next year’s midterm elections and counter President Donald Trump’s push for more partisan districts in Republican-run states.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here’s how indictment says Terry Rozier shared information that paid off for bettors
item.description: NBA guard Terry Rozier was averaging nearly 21 points per game for the Charlotte Hornets as the 2022-23 season neared an end. But during a game against the New Orleans Pelicans, he scored only 5 and played less than 10 minutes before claiming he was hurt.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Eric Adams endorses once bitter rival Andrew Cuomo in New York City's mayoral race
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed Andrew Cuomo in the city’s mayoral election Thursday, throwing his weight behind a bitter rival as he cast the Democratic nominee, Zohran Mamdani, as an avatar for radicals and gentrifiers.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deadly semitrailer crash in California renews federal criticism of immigrant truck drivers
item.description: A 21-year-old semitruck driver accused of being under the influence of drugs and causing a fiery crash that killed three people on a southern California freeway is in the country illegally, U.S. Homeland Security officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: States worry about how to fill the gap in food aid ahead of a federal benefits halt
item.description: Officials in Louisiana, Vermont and Virginia pledged Thursday to keep food aid flowing to recipients in their states, even if the federal program is stalled next month because of the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SNAP benefits may run out Nov. 1. Here's how you could still get food assistance
item.description: A disruption or delay could impact 42 million people, or 1 in 8 Americans, who rely on SNAP to buy food.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: ICE looking to hire over 40 health care workers
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is hiring for more than 40 medical positions in its immigration detention centers across the nation.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Entire White House East Wing now demolished as Trump moves forward with ballroom construction
item.description: The entire White House East Wing has been demolished as President Donald Trump moves forward with a ballroom construction, Associated Press photos on Thursday showed.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 found dead in apartment of apparent overdoses were softball teammates
item.description: Four members of the same softball team were found dead from apparent drug overdoses inside an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KRON4

item.title: Amazon hopes robots can replace 600K future hires: Report
item.description: Amazon hopes robots can replace hundreds of thousands of future roles, according to leaked documents seen by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WGN 9

item.title: Here's where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Immigrant rights group calls for removing pregnant women from detention
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Women taken into custody by U.S. immigration agents while pregnant say they received inadequate care in a letter Wednesday that calls on the Trump administration to stop holding expectant mothers in federal detention facilities. The letter to U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 times the White House underwent major renovations
item.description: President Trump's construction of a grand ballroom attached to the White House — starting with the demolition of part of the East Wing this week — will not be the first time a president has left a lasting mark on the people's house.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Task force aims to move those displaced by ex-typhoon in Alaska from shelters to hotels, rentals
item.description: Officials have identified more than 1,000 available hotel rooms plus additional rental options in Alaska's largest city for the hundreds of people displaced to Anchorage after the remnants of a typhoon ravaged their villages earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israeli settlers beat Palestinian farmers on video as attacks mount during West Bank olive harvest
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — Israeli settlers descended on Palestinian olive harvesters and activists this week in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, beating them with clubs in an attack Palestinian health officials said sent at least one woman to the hospital with serious injuries.
item.dc:creator: JALAL BWAITEL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Democrats look to long term as North Carolina GOP redistricting plan seeks another seat for Trump
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Democrats rallied Tuesday against a new U.S. House map proposed by North Carolina Republicans that would likely reap another GOP seat at President Donald Trump's behest, but the critics acknowledged they'll likely be unable to halt the redraw for now.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA and GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court overturns conviction of Colombian ex-President Uribe for bribery and witness tampering
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — An appeals court on Tuesday overturned the conviction of former Colombian President Álvaro Uribe for bribery and witness tampering for which he had been sentenced to 12 years of house arrest. Uribe, 73, has denied any wrongdoing.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A look at the US military's unusually large force in the Caribbean Sea
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has built up an unusually large force in the Caribbean Sea and the waters off the coast of Venezuela since this summer, when the Trump administration first began to shift assets to the region as part of its so-called war against narcoterrorism.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One of the world's rarest whales that makes the Atlantic its home grows in population
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — One of the rarest whales on the planet has continued an encouraging trend of population growth in the wake of new efforts to protect the giant animals, according to scientists who study them.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dems demand Trump pull watchdog office nominee after reported 'racist' texts
item.description: A new report exposed text messages in which Paul Ingrassia purportedly denounced the Martin Luther King Jr. holiday and admitted to having a “Nazi streak in me from time to time.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Walmart's 'Thanksgiving Meal Basket' will feed 10 people for under $40, company claims
item.description: Walmart made its 2025 “Thanksgiving Meal Basket” available for purchase at its lowest price since beginning the promotion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Pilots mistakenly thought someone was trying to breach cockpit during American Airlines flight
item.description: A Los Angeles-bound flight made an emergency landing on Monday after its pilots mistakenly thought someone was trying to breach the cockpit when its flight attendants banged on the locked door because the plane’s communication system went down.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Government shutdown imperils dozens of Head Start preschool programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Head Start programs that serve tens of thousands of the nation's neediest preschoolers are facing a cutoff of federal funding at the end of the month because of the government shutdown, leaving many scrambling to figure out how to keep their doors open.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: France's former President Sarkozy will begin serving a 5-year prison sentence Tuesday
item.description: Nicolas Sarkozy will become the first former French president in living memory to be imprisoned when he is expected to begin a five-year sentence Tuesday in Paris’ La Santé prison.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY SCHAEFFER and NICOLAS GARRIGA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Windshield cracks midflight, diverting United Airlines plane; officials investigating
item.description: Federal officials are investigating after a plane's windshield cracked in-flight over Utah last week.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, ABC 4
